active_ingredients: Carbamide peroxide 6.5% in anhydrous glycerin - earwax-removal ear drops (e.g., Debrox, Murine Ear)
indications_and_usage: Uses: for occasional use to help remove excessive earwax (cerumen); the carbamide peroxide releases oxygen when it contacts earwax, which softens, loosens, and helps break up the wax so it can flush or fall out, sometimes with a gentle warm-water rinse using a bulb syringe.
contraindications: Do not use if you have ear drainage or discharge, ear pain, an irritation or rash in the ear, dizziness, an ear injury or perforation (a hole in the eardrum), or recent ear surgery, without first consulting a doctor; do not use if you have a ventilation/tympanostomy tube in the eardrum; hypersensitivity to any ingredient.
warnings_and_cautions: For use in the ear only. Avoid getting it in the eyes. Do not use for more than 4 days; if excessive earwax remains after use, consult a doctor. Stop use and ask a doctor if you get ear pain, irritation, redness, swelling, or rash, or if dizziness or any drainage occurs. You may feel a crackling or bubbling sensation in the ear - that is the product working. Do not use cotton swabs to dig wax out (this pushes it deeper and can injure the canal/eardrum).
adverse_reactions: Temporary crackling/bubbling sound, mild fizzing, slight ear fullness; rarely local irritation, redness, or, with overuse, dermatitis of the canal.
dosage_and_administration: Adults and children 12 years and over: tilt the head sideways and place 5 to 10 drops into the affected ear; keep the drops in the ear for several minutes by keeping the head tilted or by placing cotton in the ear; use twice daily for up to 4 days if needed; any wax remaining after treatment may be removed by gently flushing the ear with warm water using a soft rubber bulb ear syringe. Children under 12 years: ask a doctor.
overdosage: Not expected to be harmful; ingestion of small amounts may cause GI upset and mild irritation. Contact Poison Control (1-800-222-1222) for large ingestions.
